summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on September 14, 2021, featuring a drone photography presentation from Cincy Drone Pilot for potential township marketing and social media use. The board handled multiple fire department resignations, approved a CDBG road widening project for Charles Snyder Road at State Route 28, accepted American Rescue Plan funds of $854,611, and approved promotions within the fire department. New blue and white township street signs were also approved.
decisions: Adopted tonight's agenda
Approved payment of bills from August 11 through September 14, 2021
Declared surplus and authorized sale of 2001 Case loader backhoe on GovDeals.com
Approved buyback of cemetery grave from Jill Side at Goshen Cemetery Section 2 Lot 43 Grade 5 for $450
Accepted four Skyline gift cards from Jamie Ash for police department
Accepted eight Starbucks gift cards valued at $40 from unknown donor for police department
Approved training opportunity (details partially unclear from transcript)
Approved Detective McAllister to attend Digital DNA training in Columbus, Ohio (no cost)
Rescinded 2004 resolution regarding enforcement of loud car stereos in neighborhoods
Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Travis Gilkinson effective August 11, 2021
Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Troy Myrick retroactive to April 28, 2021
Accepted resignation of volunteer firefighter Henry Overbeck effective September 7, 2021
Accepted resignation of volunteer firefighter Matt Ailer effective August 19, 2021
Declared surplus and authorized sale of 1983 Onan generator on GovDeals.com
Authorized Fire Chief Steve Pegram to terminate contract with US Acute Care Solutions for medical direction effective 60 days from notification
Approved contract with EMS Doctors LLC (Dr. Charlton) for medical director services at $4,000 every six months
Approved Resolution R-021-2021 accepting tax budget amounts and rates for fiscal year 2022
Approved Resolution R-022-2021 establishing Fund 2272 for Local Fiscal Recovery Funds
Approved increase of Fund 2272 revenue line in the amount of $854,611
Approved out-of-town travel for Fire Chief/Administrator to attend Congressional Fire Service Caucus dinner in Washington DC October 1-3 at no cost
Waived zoning permit fees for Goshen Township Historical Society Museum sign replacement
Awarded CDBG contract for Charles Snyder Road widening and turn lanes at SR 28 to Rack and Baller Excavating for $166,498.75
Approved new blue with white background street sign design (bottom right option)
Approved promotion of firefighter paramedic to rank of fire lieutenant effective/retroactive September 12, 2021
Approved promotion of fire lieutenant to rank of fire and EMS captain effective/retroactive September 12, 2021
action_items: Steve Allgaier and Cheryl to schedule follow-up meeting with Cincy Drone Pilot to discuss contract and budget for township marketing videos
Chad Meadows to begin ordering new blue/white street signs and develop replacement plan over 2-3 years with cost analysis
Steve Allgaier to send emails to state/federal counterparts regarding ARP fund usage guidance for townships
Board members to attend County Township Association meeting Thursday at fairgrounds at 6 PM to pick trick-or-treat date
Deputy Chief Myers and fire department to coordinate with park board on demolition of park house including EPA inspections and potential live fire training
Fire Chief Pegram to terminate contract with US Acute Care Solutions and execute new contract with Dr. Charlton/EMS Doctors LLC
Harry and Steve to continue working on Smith Road and SR 28 traffic light project with developer and City of Davis
key_amounts: $450 - cemetery grave buyback from Jill Side
$40 - Starbucks gift cards from unknown donor
$854,611.02 - first payment of American Rescue Plan Local Fiscal Recovery Funds
Approximately $1.6 million total expected ARP funds (two payments)
$4,000 every six months - medical director contract with Dr. Charlton/EMS Doctors LLC
$166,498.75 - CDBG contract to Rack and Baller Excavating for Charles Snyder Road widening
$41,000 - abandoned property on Linton Road sold at auction
notable_topics: Cincy Drone Pilot presentation for township marketing and social media content
American Rescue Plan funds received - usage restrictions and guidance still evolving
Charles Snyder Road and SR 28 intersection widening project (CDBG funded)
Smith Road and SR 28 traffic light progress update
Medical director contract transition from US Acute Care Solutions to EMS Doctors LLC
Rescinding outdated 2004 loud car stereo enforcement resolution
New township street sign design approved (blue with white background)
Fire conference to be hosted in Goshen Township in November
Fire/EMS awards and recognition banquet praised by trustees
Abandoned Linton Road property sold to new owners
notes: Executive session held from 7:58 PM to 8:34 PM to discuss appointment, employment, and compensation of public employees, resulting in two fire department promotions. Names of promoted individuals were not stated publicly. The township is exploring drone videography for marketing purposes but no contract was finalized. Commissioner Painter was noted as being appointed president of the National Association of County Commissioners. The part-time admin assistant position was converted to full-time split between zoning and administration. Four medic apprentices recently graduated.
title: BOT Mtg 9.14.21.mp4
